title: reverse nursing side after starting one doesn’t work
body:
## 🐛 Bug Report

reverse nursing side after starting one doesn’t work. It just closes the view.

---

**Category:** `bug` `feeding`
**Source:** Telegram Bot
**Status:** Pending implementation

> _This issue was created automatically via the BabyBloom Telegram Bot. No private baby data is included._
enhanced_description:
The "reverse nursing side" button in the active nursing/feeding timer view closes the view instead of swapping the side.
- Affected component: Feeding/nursing timer UI in src/ (likely a feeding or nursing component)
- Expected: Tapping "reverse side" while a nursing session is active should swap from L→R or R→L and continue the timer
- Current: Tapping the button closes/dismisses the entire nursing view
- Edge cases: Should work mid-session without losing elapsed time; should update the side indicator correctly
- Acceptance criteria: Reversing nursing side mid-session swaps the displayed side without closing the view or resetting the timer
implementation_notes:
Files changed:
- src/features/feeding/useFeedingTimer.ts: Added switchFeedSide() function to interface and implementation
- src/tabs/HomeTab.tsx: Added switchFeedSide() function that atomically saves current side and starts new timer; updated companion items to use it instead of stopFeedTimer()+startFeedTimer() pattern

Approach: The root cause was that stopFeedTimer() sets feedTimerApp to null via async React state, so the immediately-following startFeedTimer() check (if feedTimer return) still saw the old non-null value. switchFeedSide() saves the current session and atomically sets the new timer type without any null intermediate state.

title: it has been quite some time I created the account but the red indicators on q...
body:
## 🐛 Bug Report

it has been quite some time I created the account but the red indicators on quick log is not coming

---

**Category:** `bug`
**Source:** Telegram Bot
**Status:** Pending implementation

> _This issue was created automatically via the BabyBloom Telegram Bot. No private baby data is included._
enhanced_description:
Quick log buttons on the home screen are missing their red indicator dots (which show recent activity or pending items).
- Affected component: Quick log section on the home page (likely a QuickLog or HomeScreen component in src/)
- Expected: Red indicator dots should appear on quick log items after some time of account creation to show activity status
- Current: No red indicators appear even after extended account usage
- Edge cases: May be related to data freshness checks, timestamp comparisons, or IndexedDB query issues
- Acceptance criteria: Red indicators appear on quick log buttons when there is relevant recent activity to highlight
implementation_notes:
Files changed:
- src/tabs/HomeTab.tsx: Updated quickLogWarnings logic to use birth date as reference when no logs exist for a category

Approach: When lastMs === 0 (no entries ever logged), instead of setting warnings to null, the code now calculates hours since birth and applies the same warn/danger thresholds using the neverMsg text. This ensures red indicators appear for long-inactive accounts.

title: welcome caurasel doesn’t reflect the latest changes
body:
## 🐛 Bug Report

welcome caurasel doesn’t reflect the latest changes. I should also have demo for long press reveal additional info of quick log

---

**Category:** `bug` `content`
**Source:** Telegram Bot
**Status:** Pending implementation

> _This issue was created automatically via the BabyBloom Telegram Bot. No private baby data is included._
enhanced_description:
The welcome carousel/onboarding screens do not show the latest app features and are missing a demo for the long-press quick log feature.
- Affected component: Welcome/onboarding carousel component in src/
- Expected: Carousel should reflect current app features and include a slide demonstrating long-press to reveal additional quick log info
- Current: Carousel content is outdated and missing the long-press demo
- Edge cases: Should work on both first install and if user revisits; carousel content should be maintainable
- Acceptance criteria: Welcome carousel includes updated feature descriptions and a slide showing the long-press quick log interaction
implementation_notes:
Files changed:
- src/components/onboarding/WelcomeCarousel.tsx: Added long-press info panel to MiniDemo with history and tips; updated MockBtn to support onTouchStart/onTouchEnd; updated slide description to mention long-press feature; hid legend when info panel is open

Approach: Added long-press detection (500ms timeout) to MockBtn in the demo. When triggered, shows an info panel with mock history entries and tips for the pressed button, matching the actual app behavior.
